---
title: "Badge"
description: "Displays a badge or a component that looks like a badge."
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
library(shiny)
pkgload::load_all("..")
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# Badge

Displays a badge or a component that looks like a badge.

## Usage

```r
pkgload::load_all("..")

block_badge("Badge")
```

## Examples

### Basic

A standard badge with default styling.

<div class="sb-component-preview">
```{r, echo=FALSE, results="asis"}
cat(as.character(block_badge("Badge")))
```
</div>
<div class="sb-component-code-title">Code</div>
```r
block_badge("Badge")
```

### Variants

Use the `variant` prop to change the visual style of the badge.

<div class="sb-component-preview">
```{r, echo=FALSE, results="asis"}
cat(as.character(htmltools::tagList(
  block_badge("Default"),
  block_badge("Secondary", variant = "secondary"),
  block_badge("Outline", variant = "outline"),
  block_badge("Destructive", variant = "destructive")
)))
```
</div>
<div class="sb-component-code-title">Code</div>
```r
block_badge("Default")
block_badge("Secondary", variant = "secondary")
block_badge("Outline", variant = "outline")
block_badge("Destructive", variant = "destructive")
```
